common.name: Name
common.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters

login.title: Sign In
login.subtitle: Sign in to manage your PDS account
login.infoSavedAccountsDesc: Click an account to sign in instantly. Your session tokens are stored securely in this browser.
login.infoNewAccountDesc: Use the sign-in button to add a different account. Click the × to remove saved accounts from this browser.
login.infoSecureSignInDesc: You'll be redirected to authenticate securely. If you have passkeys or two-factor authentication enabled, you'll be prompted for those too.
login.infoStaySignedInDesc: After signing in, your account will be saved to this browser for quick access next time.
login.infoRecoveryDesc: Lost your password or passkey? Use the recovery links below the sign-in button.

verification.title: Verify Your Account
verification.subtitle: Your account needs verification. Enter the code sent to your verification method.

register.title: Create Account
register.subtitle: Create a new account on this PDS
register.subtitleKeyChoice: Choose how to set up your external did:web identity.
register.subtitleInitialDidDoc: Upload your DID document to continue.
register.subtitleUpdatedDidDoc: Update your DID document with the PDS signing key.
register.subtitleComplete: Your account has been created successfully!
register.infoIdentityDesc: Your identity determines how your account is identified across the ATProto network. Most users should choose the standard option.
register.infoContactDesc: We'll use this to verify your account and send important notifications about your account security.
register.infoNextDesc: After creating your account, you'll verify your contact method and then you're ready to use any ATProto app with your new identity.
register.migrateDescription: You can migrate your existing account to this PDS instead of creating a new one. Your followers, posts, and identity will come with you.
register.handleDotWarning: Custom domain handles can be set up after account creation in Settings.
register.identityHint: Choose how your decentralized identity will be managed.
register.didPlcHint: Portable identity managed by PLC Directory
register.didWebHint: Identity hosted on this PDS (read warning below)
register.didWebDisabledHint: Not available on this PDS - use did:plc or bring your own did:web
register.didWebWarning1Detail: Your identity will be {did}. Even if you migrate to another PDS later, this server must continue hosting your DID document.
register.didWebWarning2Detail: Unlike did:plc, did:web has no rotation keys. If this PDS goes offline permanently, your identity cannot be recovered.
register.didWebWarning3Detail: If you migrate away, we will continue serving a minimal DID document pointing to your new PDS. Your identity will remain functional.
register.didWebWarning4Detail: Choose did:plc unless you have a specific reason to prefer did:web.
register.externalDidHint: Your domain must serve a valid DID document at /.well-known/did.json pointing to this PDS
register.contactMethodHint: Choose how you'd like to verify your account and receive notifications. You only need one.
register.discordIdHint: Your numeric Discord user ID (enable Developer Mode to find it)

validation.handleNoDots: Handle cannot contain dots. You can set up a custom domain handle after creating your account.
validation.passwordLength: Password must be at least 8 characters
validation.externalDidFormat: External DID must start with did:web:
validation.emailRequired: Email is required for email verification
validation.discordIdRequired: Discord ID is required for Discord verification
validation.telegramRequired: Telegram username is required for Telegram verification
validation.signalRequired: Phone number is required for Signal verification

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.deactivatedMessage: Your account is currently deactivated. This typically happens during account migration. Some features may be limited until your account is reactivated.
dashboard.navSessionsDesc: View and manage your login sessions
dashboard.navRepoDesc: Browse and manage raw AT Protocol records
dashboard.navDelegationDesc: Manage account controllers and delegated accounts
dashboard.navDidDocumentDesc: Manage your DID document for external migrations
dashboard.navBackupDesc: Download your repository as a CAR file
dashboard.migratedMessage: Your account has migrated to {pds}. Your DID document is still hosted here, and you can update it for future migrations.
dashboard.navMigrateAgainDesc: Move to another PDS and update your DID document

didEditor.title: DID Document Editor
didEditor.verificationMethodsDesc: Signing keys that can act on behalf of your DID. When you migrate to a new PDS, add their signing key here.
didEditor.noKeys: No verification methods configured. Using the local PDS key.
didEditor.alsoKnownAsDesc: Handles that point to your DID. Update this when your handle changes on a new PDS.
didEditor.noHandles: No handles configured. Using the local handle.
didEditor.serviceEndpointDesc: The PDS that currently hosts your account data. Update this when migrating.
didEditor.invalidMultibase: Public key must be a valid multibase string starting with 'z'
didEditor.invalidHandle: Handle must be an at:// URI (e.g., at://handle.example.com)
didEditor.helpText: When you migrate to another PDS, that PDS generates new signing keys. Update your DID document here so it points to your new keys and location. This enables multi-hop migrations (PDS 1 → PDS 2 → PDS 3).

settings.title: Account Settings
settings.customDomainDescription: Use your own domain as your handle. You need to verify domain ownership first.
settings.setupMethodsIntro: Choose one of these verification methods:
settings.dnsMethodDesc: Add this TXT record to your domain:
settings.httpMethodDesc: Serve your DID at this URL:
settings.exportDataDescription: Download your entire repository as a CAR (Content Addressable Archive) file. This includes all your posts, likes, follows, and other data.

backups.title: Backups
backups.description: Your repository is automatically backed up daily. You can also create manual backups or restore from a previous backup.
backups.restoreDescription: Upload a CAR file to restore your repository. This will overwrite your current data.

settings.deleteWarning: This action is irreversible. All your data will be permanently deleted.

messages.emailCodeSent: Verification code sent to your notification channel
messages.emailCodeSentToCurrent: Verification code sent to your current email address
messages.passwordLength: Password must be at least 8 characters
messages.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
messages.deletionCodeSent: Deletion confirmation sent to your email
messages.deletionConfirmationSent: Deletion confirmation sent to your email
messages.deleteConfirmation: Are you absolutely sure you want to delete your account? This cannot be undone.
messages.confirmDelete: Are you absolutely sure you want to delete your account? This cannot be undone.

appPasswords.title: App Passwords
appPasswords.description: App passwords let you sign in to third-party apps without giving them your main password. Each app password can be revoked individually.
appPasswords.createdMessage: Copy this password now. You won't be able to see it again.
appPasswords.revokeConfirm: Revoke app password "{name}"? Apps using this password will no longer be able to access your account.
appPasswords.saveWarningMessage: This password is required to sign into apps that don't support passkeys or OAuth. You will only see it once.
appPasswords.acknowledgeLabel: I have saved my app password in a secure location

sessions.title: Active Sessions
sessions.revokeCurrentConfirm: This will log you out of this session. Continue?
sessions.revokeAllConfirm: This will revoke {count} other session(s). Continue?

inviteCodes.title: Invite Codes
inviteCodes.description: Invite codes let you invite friends to join. Each code can be used once.
inviteCodes.disableConfirm: Disable this invite code? It can no longer be used.

security.title: Security
security.passkeysDescription: Passkeys provide secure, passwordless authentication using your device's built-in security (fingerprint, face, or PIN).
security.deletePasskeyConfirm: Delete passkey "{name}"? You won't be able to use it to sign in anymore.
security.totpDescription: Use an authenticator app like Google Authenticator, Authy, or 1Password for two-factor authentication.
security.totpSetupInstructions: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app, then enter the 6-digit code to verify.
security.backupCodesDescription: Use these codes to sign in if you lose access to your authenticator app. Each code can only be used once.
security.regenerateConfirm: Regenerate backup codes? Your current codes will no longer work.
security.legacyLoginDescription: Allow signing in with username/password directly (legacy mode). When disabled, you must use OAuth with MFA.
security.legacyLoginWarning: Warning: Enabling legacy login bypasses MFA for direct password logins. Only enable if needed for app compatibility.
security.totpPasswordWarning: With TOTP enabled, changing your password from the Bluesky app (or other legacy apps) will be blocked. To change your password, you have two options:
security.totpPasswordOption1Suffix: where you can verify with your authenticator app.
security.totpPasswordOption2Suffix: to verify your Bluesky session with TOTP, then password changes will work temporarily.
security.legacyAppsDescription: Some apps (like the official Bluesky app) use older authentication that only requires your password. When you have MFA enabled, these apps bypass your second factor. Disabling legacy login forces all apps to use OAuth, which properly enforces MFA.
security.removePasswordConfirm: Remove your password? You'll need to use passkeys to sign in.
security.passwordDescription: Manage your account password. If you have passkeys set up, you can optionally remove your password for a fully passwordless experience.
security.disableTotpWarning: This will make your account less secure.
security.removePasswordWarning: This will make your account passkey-only. You'll only be able to sign in using your registered passkeys. If you lose access to all your passkeys, you can recover your account using your notification channel.
security.beforeProceedingItem1: Make sure you have at least one reliable passkey registered
security.beforeProceedingItem2: Consider registering passkeys on multiple devices
security.beforeProceedingItem3: Ensure your recovery notification channel is up to date
security.addPasskeyFirst: Add at least one passkey before you can remove your password.
security.passkeyOnlyHint: You sign in using passkeys only. If you ever lose access to your passkeys, you can recover your account using the "Lost passkey?" link on the login page.
security.trustedDevicesDescription: Manage devices that can skip two-factor authentication when signing in. Trust is granted for 30 days and automatically extends when you use the device.
security.sessionExpired: Session expired. Please log in again.
security.legacyLoginDisabled: Legacy app login disabled - only OAuth apps can sign in
security.passwordRemoved: Password removed. Your account is now passkey-only.
security.passkeysNotSupported: Passkeys are not supported in this browser

comms.title: Communication Preferences
comms.description: Choose how you want to receive important messages like password resets, security alerts, and account updates.
comms.preferredChannelDescription: Select your preferred way to receive messages. You must configure a channel before you can select it.
comms.emailManagedInSettings: Your email is managed in Account Settings
comms.discordIdHint: Your Discord user ID (not username). Enable Developer Mode in Discord to copy it.
comms.telegramHint: Your Telegram username without the @ symbol
comms.signalHint: Your Signal phone number with country code
comms.historyDescription: View recent messages sent to your account.

repoExplorer.title: Repository Explorer
repoExplorer.description: Browse and manage your raw AT Protocol records.
repoExplorer.deleteConfirm: Delete record {rkey}? This cannot be undone.
repoExplorer.noCollectionsYet: No collections yet. Create your first record to get started.
repoExplorer.autoGeneratedHint: Leave empty to auto-generate a TID-based key
repoExplorer.demoPostText: Hello from my PDS! This is my first post.

admin.title: Admin Panel
admin.serverNameHelp: Displayed in the browser tab and other places
admin.logoHelp: Used as favicon and shown in the navbar
admin.themeColorsHint: Leave blank to use default colors.
admin.deleteConfirm: Delete account @{handle}? This cannot be undone.

login.title: Sign In
login.subtitle: Sign in to continue to the application
login.passkeyHintNotAvailable: No passkeys registered for this account
login.passkeyHint: Use your device's biometrics or security key

consent.title: Authorize Application
consent.permissions: This application will be able to:
consent.writePosts: Create and delete posts on your behalf
consent.rememberChoiceLabel: Remember my choice for this application

twoFactorCode.title: Two-Factor Authentication
twoFactorCode.subtitle: A verification code has been sent to your {channel}. Enter the code below to continue.

totp.title: Enter Authenticator Code
totp.subtitle: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app
totp.hintDefault: 6 digits for authenticator, 8 characters for backup code

passkey.title: Passkey Verification
passkey.subtitle: Use your passkey to verify your identity

verify.title: Verify Your Account
verify.subtitle: We've sent a verification code to your {channel}. Enter it below to complete registration.
verify.tokenSubtitle: Enter the verification code and the identifier it was sent to.
verify.codeHelp: Copy the entire code from your message, including dashes
verify.codeResentDetail: Verification code sent! Check your inbox.
verify.startOver: Start over with a different account
verify.noPendingInfo: If you recently created an account and need to verify it, you may need to create a new account. If you already verified your account, you can sign in.
verify.canNowSignIn: You can now sign in to your account.
verify.migrationContinue: You can close this tab and continue your migration in the original window.
verify.identifierHelp: The email address or identifier the code was sent to
verify.emailUpdateSubtitle: Enter your new email address and the verification code sent to your current email.
verify.emailUpdateRequiresAuth: You must be signed in to update your email address.
verify.emailUpdateCodeHelp: The code was sent to your current email address
verify.emailUpdated: Your email has been updated successfully.
verify.emailUpdatedInfo: You may need to verify your new email address.

resetPassword.title: Reset Password
resetPassword.subtitle: Enter the code you received and choose a new password.
resetPassword.forgotSubtitle: Enter your handle or email and we'll send you a code to reset your password.
resetPassword.codeSent: Password reset code sent! Check your preferred notification channel.
resetPassword.enterCode: Enter the code you received and your new password.
resetPassword.passwordLength: Password must be at least 8 characters

recoverPasskey.title: Recover Your Account
recoverPasskey.invalidLinkMessage: This recovery link is invalid or has been corrupted. Please request a new recovery email.
recoverPasskey.successMessage: Your temporary password has been set. You can now sign in with this password.
recoverPasskey.successNextSteps: After signing in, we recommend adding a new passkey in your security settings to restore passkey-only authentication.
recoverPasskey.subtitle: Set a temporary password to regain access to your passkey-only account.
recoverPasskey.whatHappensNextDetail: After setting this password, you can sign in and add a new passkey in your security settings. Once you have a new passkey, you can optionally remove the temporary password.

validation.passwordLength: Password must be at least 8 characters

errors.invalidLink: Invalid recovery link. Please request a new one.
errors.expired: This recovery link has expired. Please request a new one.

requestPasskeyRecovery.title: Recover Passkey Account
requestPasskeyRecovery.subtitle: Lost access to your passkey? Enter your handle or email and we'll send you a recovery link.
requestPasskeyRecovery.successMessage: If your account exists and is a passkey-only account, you'll receive a recovery link at your preferred notification channel.
requestPasskeyRecovery.successInfo: The link will expire in 1 hour. Check your email, Discord, Telegram, or Signal depending on your account settings.
requestPasskeyRecovery.howItWorksDetail: We'll send a secure link to your registered notification channel. Click the link to set a temporary password. Then you can sign in and add a new passkey.

registerPasskey.title: Create Passkey Account
registerPasskey.subtitle: Create an ultra-secure account using a passkey instead of a password.
registerPasskey.subtitleKeyChoice: Choose how to set up your external did:web identity.
registerPasskey.subtitleInitialDidDoc: Upload your DID document to continue.
registerPasskey.subtitlePasskey: Register your passkey to secure your account.
registerPasskey.subtitleAppPassword: Save your app password for third-party apps.
registerPasskey.subtitleUpdatedDidDoc: Update your DID document with the PDS signing key.
registerPasskey.subtitleComplete: Your account has been created successfully!
registerPasskey.handleDotWarning: Custom domain handles can be set up after account creation.
registerPasskey.contactMethodHint: Choose how you'd like to verify your account and receive notifications.
registerPasskey.identityTypeHint: Choose how your decentralized identity will be managed.
registerPasskey.didPlcHint: Portable identity managed by PLC Directory
registerPasskey.didWebHint: Identity hosted on this PDS (read warning below)
registerPasskey.didWebDisabledHint: Not available on this PDS - use did:plc or bring your own did:web
registerPasskey.didWebWarning2Detail: Unlike did:plc, did:web has no rotation keys.
registerPasskey.didWebWarning3Detail: If you migrate away, we will continue serving a minimal DID document.
registerPasskey.didWebWarning4Detail: Choose did:plc unless you have a specific reason to prefer did:web.
registerPasskey.externalDidHint: You'll need to serve a DID document at
registerPasskey.whyPasskeyOnlyDesc: Passkey accounts are more secure than password-based accounts because they:
registerPasskey.whyPasskeyBullet1: Cannot be phished or stolen in data breaches
registerPasskey.whyPasskeyBullet3: Require your biometric or device PIN to use
registerPasskey.infoWhyPasskeyDesc: Passkeys are cryptographic credentials stored on your device. They cannot be phished, guessed, or stolen in data breaches like passwords can.
registerPasskey.infoHowItWorksDesc: When you sign in, your device will prompt you to verify with Face ID, Touch ID, or your device PIN. No password to remember or type.
registerPasskey.infoAppAccessDesc: After creating your account, you will receive an app password. Use this to sign in to Bluesky apps and other AT Protocol clients.
registerPasskey.passkeyNameHint: A friendly name to identify this passkey
registerPasskey.passkeyPrompt: Click the button below to create your passkey. You'll be prompted to use:
registerPasskey.passkeyPromptBullet3: A security key (if you have one)

errors.handleNoDots: Handle cannot contain dots. You can set up a custom domain handle after creating your account.
errors.externalDidFormat: External DID must start with did:web:
errors.emailRequired: Email is required for email verification
errors.discordRequired: Discord ID is required for Discord verification
errors.telegramRequired: Telegram username is required for Telegram verification
errors.signalRequired: Phone number is required for Signal verification
errors.passkeysNotSupported: Passkeys are not supported in this browser. Please use a different browser or register with a password instead.

trustedDevices.title: Trusted Devices
trustedDevices.description: Trusted devices can skip two-factor authentication when logging in. Trust is granted for 30 days and automatically extends when you use the device.
trustedDevices.noDevicesHint: When you log in with two-factor authentication enabled, you can choose to trust the device for 30 days.
trustedDevices.revokeConfirm: Are you sure you want to revoke trust for this device? You will need to enter your 2FA code next time you log in from this device.

reauth.title: Re-authentication Required
reauth.subtitle: Please verify your identity to continue.
reauth.passkeyPrompt: Click the button below to authenticate with your passkey.

delegation.title: Account Delegation
delegation.controllersDesc: Accounts that can act on your behalf
delegation.noControllers: No controllers have been granted access to your account.
delegation.removeConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove this controller?
delegation.cannotAddControllers: You cannot add controllers because this account controls other accounts. An account can either have controllers or control other accounts, but not both.
delegation.controlledAccountsDesc: Accounts you can act on behalf of
delegation.noControlledAccounts: You do not have access to any delegated accounts.
delegation.cannotControlAccounts: You cannot control other accounts because this account has controllers. An account can either have controllers or control other accounts, but not both.

actAs.noAccess: You do not have access to this account
actAs.title: Act As

oauthDelegation.title: Delegated Account
oauthDelegation.enterControllerHandle: Sign in with your controller account to access this account.
oauthDelegation.controllerNotFound: Account not found or you don't have access to this delegated account
oauthDelegation.authenticateAs: Authenticate as {controller} to act on behalf of {delegated}

oauthConsent.readOnlyDesc: View public information only. No write access to this account.
oauthConsent.permissionsLimitedDesc: Your actual permissions will be limited to your {level} access level, regardless of what the app requests.
oauthConsent.viewerLimitedDesc: As a Viewer, you have read-only access. This app will not be able to create, update, or delete content on this account.
oauthConsent.editorLimitedDesc: As an Editor, you can create and edit content but cannot manage account settings or security.

verifyChannel.title: Verify Channel
verifyChannel.subtitle: Enter the verification code sent to your notification channel.
verifyChannel.signInRequiredDesc: You must be signed in to verify a channel.
verifyChannel.pleaseWait: Please wait while we verify your channel.
verifyChannel.identifierHelp: The email address, Discord ID, Telegram username, or Signal number being verified.
verifyChannel.codeHelp: Copy the entire code from your message, including dashes.

migration.title: Account Migration
migration.subtitle: Move your AT Protocol identity between servers
migration.navDesc: Move your account to or from another PDS
migration.migrateHereDesc: Move your existing AT Protocol account to this PDS from another server.
migration.whatIsMigrationDesc: Account migration allows you to move your AT Protocol identity between Personal Data Servers (PDSes). Your DID (decentralized identifier) stays the same, so your followers and social connections are preserved.
migration.beforeMigrate1: You will need your current account credentials
migration.beforeMigrate2: Migration requires email verification for security
migration.beforeMigrate3: Large accounts with many images may take several minutes
migration.beforeMigrate4: Your old PDS will be notified to deactivate your account
migration.importantWarning: Account migration is a significant action. Make sure you trust the destination PDS and understand that your data will be moved. If something goes wrong, recovery may require manual intervention.
migration.offlineRestoreDesc: Restore from backup when your old PDS is unavailable.

resume.title: Resume Migration?
resume.incomplete: You have an incomplete migration in progress:
resume.reenterCredentials: You will need to re-enter your credentials to continue.

welcome.title: Migrate to This PDS
welcome.desc: Move your existing AT Protocol account to this server.
welcome.understand: I understand the risks and want to proceed

sourceAuth.title: Enter Your Current Handle
sourceAuth.desc: Enter the handle of the account you want to migrate.
sourceAuth.descResume: Re-authenticate to your source PDS to continue the migration.
sourceAuth.handleHint: Your current handle on your existing PDS
sourceAuth.resumeOAuthNote: You need to re-authenticate via OAuth to continue.

chooseHandle.title: Choose Your New Handle
chooseHandle.desc: Select a handle for your account on this PDS.
chooseHandle.handleHint: You can also use your own domain by entering the full handle (e.g., alice.mydomain.com)
chooseHandle.authPasskeyDesc: Passwordless login using biometrics or security key
chooseHandle.passkeyInfo: You'll set up a passkey after your account is created. Your device will prompt you to use biometrics (fingerprint, Face ID) or a security key.

review.title: Review Migration
review.desc: Please confirm the details of your migration.
review.warning: After you click "Start Migration", your repository and data will begin transferring. This process cannot be easily undone.

migrating.title: Migration in Progress
migrating.desc: Please wait while your account is being transferred...

passkeySetup.title: Set Up Your Passkey
passkeySetup.desc: Your email has been verified. Now set up your passkey for secure, passwordless login.
passkeySetup.nameHint: A friendly name to identify this passkey
passkeySetup.instructions: Click the button below to register your passkey. Your device will prompt you to use biometrics (fingerprint, Face ID) or a security key.

appPassword.title: Save Your App Password
appPassword.desc: Your passkey has been created. An app password has been generated for you to use with apps that don't support passkeys yet.
appPassword.warning: This app password is required to sign into apps that don't support passkeys yet (like bsky.app). You will only see this password once.
appPassword.label: App Password for
appPassword.saved: I have saved my app password in a secure location

emailVerify.title: Verify Your Email
emailVerify.desc: A verification code has been sent to {email}.
emailVerify.hint: Enter the code below, or click the link in the email to continue automatically.

plcToken.title: Verify Migration
plcToken.desc: A verification code has been sent to the email registered with your old account.
plcToken.info: This code confirms you have access to the account and authorizes updating your identity to point to this PDS.

didWebUpdate.title: Update Your DID Document
didWebUpdate.desc: Since you're using a did:web identity, you need to update your DID document to point to this PDS.
didWebUpdate.updateInstructions: Update the did.json file at your domain to point the atproto_pds service endpoint to this PDS:
didWebUpdate.verifyFirst: Make sure your DID document is updated and publicly accessible before completing the migration.

finalizing.title: Finalizing Migration
finalizing.desc: Please wait while we complete the migration...

success.title: Migration Complete!
success.desc: Your account has been successfully migrated to this PDS.
success.blobsWarning: {count} blobs could not be migrated. These may be images or other media that are no longer available.

common.step1: Log in to your current PDS
common.step2: Choose your new handle on this server
common.step3: Your repository and blobs will be transferred
common.step5: Your identity will be updated to point here
common.warning1: You need access to the email registered with your current account
common.warning2: Large accounts may take several minutes to transfer
common.warning3: Your old account will be deactivated after migration

welcome.title: Offline Restore
welcome.desc: Restore your account when your old PDS is unavailable. This is for disaster recovery when you cannot contact your previous server.
welcome.warningDesc: This method requires your rotation key private key. Only use this if your previous PDS has shut down or you cannot access it.
welcome.requirement2: A CAR file backup of your repository
welcome.requirement3: Your rotation key (private key in hex, base58, or JWK format)
welcome.understand: I understand this is for offline recovery only

provideDid.title: Enter Your DID
provideDid.desc: Enter the DID of the account you want to restore.
provideDid.label: Your DID

uploadCar.title: Upload Repository Backup
uploadCar.desc: Upload the CAR file containing your repository data.
uploadCar.label: CAR File
uploadCar.hint: This should be a .car file from a previous backup of your repository
uploadCar.reuploadWarning: Your session was restored, but you need to re-upload your CAR file. For security reasons, file contents are not stored between sessions.

rotationKey.title: Provide Rotation Key
rotationKey.desc: Enter your rotation key to prove ownership of this DID.
rotationKey.securityWarning1: Your rotation key is extremely sensitive - anyone with it can take over your identity
rotationKey.securityWarning2: Only enter it on trusted devices and connections
rotationKey.securityWarning3: The key will not be stored after migration
rotationKey.label: Rotation Key
rotationKey.placeholder: Paste your rotation key (hex, base58, or JWK)...
rotationKey.hint: Supports 64-character hex, base58, or JWK format
rotationKey.valid: Rotation key verified! You have control of this DID.
rotationKey.invalid: This key is not a valid rotation key for this DID.

review.desc: Please confirm the details of your offline restoration.
review.warning: After you click "Start Migration", your repository will be imported and your DID will be updated to point to this PDS.
review.plcWarning: Once you start, your DID document will be updated to point to this PDS. If something goes wrong, you can use your rotation key to recover, but you should complete the migration to avoid a broken identity state.

migrating.title: Restoring Account
migrating.desc: Please wait while your account is being restored...

blobs.title: Migrating Blobs
blobs.desc: Attempting to recover images and media from your old PDS...
blobs.failedTitle: Some blobs could not be migrated
blobs.failedDesc: {count} blobs could not be fetched from your old PDS. This may happen if the server is unreachable or the files were deleted.
blobs.sourceUnreachable: Could not connect to your old PDS to fetch media files. This is common when migrating from a shut-down server. Your posts will work, but some images may be missing.

success.desc: Your account has been successfully restored to this PDS.

errors.accountExists: Account already exists on target PDS
